description: A piece of land almost surrounded by water, connected to the mainland by a narrow strip. 'Kerone' names the reaching land—extending into water yet still connected, almost but not quite an island.
sound_symbolism: The word reaches while staying attached. 'Ke' begins connected, the land anchored. Then 'ro' reaches outward, extending into water. Finally 'ne' nearly surrounds, water on three sides.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a noun: 'lo kerone' (the peninsula). Takes the elemental classifier 'themo' when counted. Related to 'mola' (island) but maintains connection to larger land.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Peninsulas teach the middle way between isolation and connection—reaching out while remaining attached.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Peninsulas offered defensive advantages and maritime access while maintaining land connection—strategic places throughout history.
